narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after pouring your heart into your law school dreams, and I want to walk you through why our prediction model saw such a high risk—91% chance—of you struggling to pass the bar exam, leading to this tough call on your application. Picture the model piecing together your story like chapters in a book: it started with your Year 3 grades placing you in the bottom decile of your school, which stood out as the strongest signal of potential trouble ahead, suggesting the rigors of advanced legal studies might prove overwhelming. Right behind that was your Year 1 decile in the bottom as well, painting a consistent picture of early academic hurdles that didn't shift over time. Your LSAT score of 26 then layered on real concern, as it signaled challenges with the kind of analytical reasoning the bar demands. Even your upper-middle family income, while a solid foundation, nudged the outlook downward in this context, perhaps reflecting patterns where similar backgrounds haven't always bridged performance gaps. Your Asian background and undergraduate GPA of 2.3 added to the cautionary tale the model read, each pulling toward a tougher path. Being a full-time student and female rounded out the influences, all aligning in a way that made the model worry your unique combination might not equip you to conquer the bar's gauntlet. It's not about doubting your drive—it's this specific mosaic of your journey that lit up those warning lights for us. Many in similar spots have turned things around with targeted changes, like boosting study strategies or seeking mentorship. You're clearly passionate, given your aim for law school, and this could be the nudge to refine your path. Hang in there; your story isn't over. With care, The Admissions Team
